methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $11M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 15 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Xaira Therapeutics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $2.7B

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Seed), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Trilobio is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Roya Amini-Naieni and Maximilian Schommer that has raised $11M to date. The valuation framework draws on a universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in the Biotech & Health sector, including Xaira Therapeutics, Zeta Surgical, and Abridge.

Stage-progression represents the sole applicable triangulation method. Using total capital raised ($11M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier—the typical multiplier observed in transitions from seed to next stage—yields an implied valuation of $28M. Peer-median analysis could not be applied due to insufficient datapoints within narrow same-stage or adjacent-stage peer sets. Public-comp anchoring, while supported by a 6.7x median price-to-sales multiple across the three public biotech comparables, cannot be executed without revenue disclosure from Trilobio.

The single-method triangulation supports a valuation range of $23M (low) to $32M (high), with a median of $28M. This range carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method and the absence of revenue data to cross-check via public comparables. The narrowness of applicable methods underscores that seed-stage biotech valuations remain heavily dependent on capital-raised inflection points rather than operational metrics. Further validation would require either peer revenue multiples or clarity on Trilobio's post-seed funding trajectory.
